content.full_text:
Farn PolrrIcal PnecrrcEs CourrarssroN
428 J Street r Suite 620 o Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 o Fax (916) 322-0886
December 23,2003
Joanna A. Ward
CEOlPresident
Visionary Integration Professionals, Inc.
80 Iron Point Circle, Suite 100
Folsom, CA 95630
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-03-282
Dear Ms. Ward:
This letter is in response to your request for advice on behalf of Visionary
Integration Professionals, Inc. and its employee Paul Spencer regarding the conflict-of-
interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act'1.r fne Commission is charged
with interpreting and enforcing the provisions of the Act, and may provide advice onf
with respect to those provisions. (Section 83114).
QUESTTONS
1. Is Visionary Integration Professionals, Inc. ("VIP') limited in bidding on an
RFP with a state agency when the spouse of a VIP employee works for a current state
contractor involved in working on the RFp?
2. Ate there any limitations on Mr. Spencer's involvement in the contract, as an
employee of vIP, during the procurement phase of the contract?
3. Are there any limitations on Mr. Spencer's involvement, as ar employee of
VIP, during the implementation phase of the contract, if VIP is the selected iendor?
I Govemment Code sections 81000-91014. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless
otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at-2 California Code of Regulationr, .""tioo, 1g009-
18997. AII references to regulations are to Title2,Division 6 of the California Coae of n"g,rtuiion..


File No. A-03-282
Page2
CONCLUSIONS
1. The Act regulates the conduct of public officials and former public officials. It
does not impact VIP's ability to bid on a specific contract.
2. Mr. Spencer is not a public official or formerpublic official; thus the Act does
not limit his participation in procuring a contract for VIp.
3. The answer to this question will depend on whether Mr. Spencer is considered
a public official and whether VIP is the selected vendor for the contract. As a public
official, he would be prohibited from making, participating in makin g andinfluencing
any govemmental decision that will have a foreseeable and material financial effect on
any economic interest, including but not limited to sources of income as discussed below.
F'ACTS
Mr. Spencer is an employee of VIP. His spouse (Kim LaFever) is an employee of
Delegata, a competitor of VIP's.2 Delegatahas a contra.t *itt the state to prepare
requirement definitions and RFP preparation support for a systerns development project
to be undertaken by the Department. Mr. Spencer's spouse is on the Delegata team
delivering the services associated with the above-mentioned scope of work. In her staff
role, his spouse is helping the California State Deparknent define requirements, and
assisting in the RFP preparation. However, neither Delegata nor theipouse have any
authority or responsibility to make decisions or grant approvals of anykind on behalf of
the state. The role of Delegata and the spouse is to provide information, general advice,
and recommendations. All decisions are made by the Department.
on Decemb er 8,2003, you confirmed that Mr. spencer is not a designated
employee of a state agency.
ANALYSIS
The Act's conflict-of-interest provisions ensure that public officials will perform
their duties in an impartial manner, free from bias caused by their own financiallnterests
or the financial interests ofpersons who have supported them. (Section 81001(b).) The
Act does not preclude anyperson from occupyrng any governmental position, trriit -uy
bar participation in certain govemmental decisions in which an official has a conflict of
interest. Section 87100 prohibits any public official from rnaking, participating in
making, or otherwise using his orher official position to influence a govirnmental
decision in which the official has a financial interest..
A public official has a "financial interest" in a governmental decision, within the
meaning of the Act, when it is "reasonably foreseeable" that the governmental decision
will have a material financial effect on one oi more of the official's economic interests.
2 Nothing in this letter should be conskued as advice regarding Ms. LaFever's duties under the Act, since
you are not authorized to request advice on her behalf. Thus, we do not advice on her duties herein.


File No. A-03-282
Page3
(Section 87103; regulation 18700(a).) The Commissionhas adopted an eight-step
analytical framework for deciding whether apublic official has a disqualifying conflict of
interest in a given governmental decision. (Regulation 18700(b).) we apply this
analytical process to your question.
The Act's conflict-of-interest provisions apply only to "public officials."'?ublic
offrcial," for purposes of the Act, is defined to include every member, officer, employee,
or consultant of a state or local agerrcy, with certain exceptions not relevant here. (Section
82048.)
Mr. Spencer is not a member, officer, or employee of the state. Thus, the critical
question is whether he is a "consultant" under the Act. Regulation 18701(a)(2) defines
"consultant" for this purpose; it provides:
"'consultant' means an individual who, pursuant to a contract with
a state or local government agency:
"(A) Makes a govefirmental decision whether to:
"(1) Approve a rate, rule, or regulation;
"(2) Adopt or enforce alaw;
"(3) Issue, denS suspend, or revoke any permit, license,
application, certiftcate, approval, order, or similar authorization or
entitlement;
"(4) Authoizethe agency to enter into, modiff, or renew a
contract provided it is the tlpe of contract that requires ageficy approval;
"(5) Grant agenLcy approval to a contract that requires agency
approval and in which the agency is a party or to the specifications for
such a contract;
"(6) Grant agency approval to a plan, design, rqrort, study, or
similaritem;
"(7) Adopt, or grant agency approval of policies, standards, or
guidelines for the agency, or for any subdivision thereof; or
"(B) Serves in a staff capacity with the agency and in that capacity
participates in making a govemmental decision as defined in Regulation
18702.2 or performs the same or substantially all the same duties for the
agency that would otherwise be performed by an individual holding a
position specified in the agency's Conflict of Interest Code under
Government Code Secti on 87 302."


File No. A-03-282
Page 4
Based upon the facts presented in your advice request, Mr. Spencer will be
involved in procurement activity for VIP, not work in connection with an existing state or
local contract. Thus, Mr. Spencer would not be a consultant, and is not a public official
subject to the conflict of interest provisions of the Act.3
If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916)
322-5660.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel
John W. Wallace
Assistant General Counsel
Legal Division
Jwwjg

3 Note that this advice may change if VIP is awarded the contract. Mr. Spencer's role in implementing the
contract will need to be considered in determining if he is a public official.
